sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Torino, currently sitting at 10th in the league with 44 points, will host Venezia, who are struggling in 18th place with 26 points, on May 2, 2025, at 18:45. This Round 35 clash at Stadio Olimpico Grande Torino in Turin promises to be an intriguing battle. With Torino looking to solidify their mid-table position and Venezia desperate for points to avoid relegation, the stakes are high for both sides. The referee, Simone Sozza, is known for a strict approach, averaging 4.7 yellow cards and 0.17 red cards per game, which could influence the match's tempo.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at their last 5 encounters, the head-to-head record between Torino and Venezia is evenly matched, with Torino securing 1 win, 2 draws, and Venezia taking 2 victories. Their most recent meeting on August 30, 2024, saw Torino edge out a 1-0 victory away at Venezia, with defender Saúl Coco earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.2 Sofascore rating. Historically, matches between these teams have been tight, with 6 out of 7 recent games seeing both teams score, indicating a high likelihood of goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Torino have been part of low-scoring affairs recently, with 6 out of 6 games featuring under 2.5 goals, a trend that suggests a cautious approach at home. They’ve also been the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 matches, which could be a concern against a Venezia side hungry for points. Venezia, on the other hand, have also struggled to produce high-scoring games, with 8 of their last 10 matches under 2.5 goals, and they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 7 head-to-heads against Torino. Additionally, Venezia’s away games often see fewer than 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 outings, pointing to limited attacking fluidity.

sections.subtitle: Torino Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Torino’s attack will likely rely on forward Che Adams, who has scored 9 goals against an expected 8.08, showing clinical finishing with a positive xG overperformance of 0.9231. Midfielder Nikola Vlašić has also been influential, contributing 5 goals and 4 assists with a strong xG overperformance of 1.8979, making him a creative force. Defender Cristiano Biraghi stands out from past encounters against Venezia, averaging a 7.3 Sofascore rating over 3 starts, though he hasn’t scored or assisted in those games. Torino’s season stats show a total of 39 goals scored (12th in the league), with a goal distribution favoring the 31-45 minute period where they’ve netted 25% of their goals.

sections.subtitle: Venezia Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Venezia will look to Gaetano Oristanio for inspiration in attack, as the forward boasts 1.6 successful dribbles per game, ranking 5th in the league, alongside 2 goals and 3 assists. Midfielder Hans Nicolussi Caviglia has also chipped in with 4 goals and 2 assists, overperforming his expected goals by 1.7888, showing his knack for crucial moments. Goalkeeper Filip Stanković has been in form over the last 5 matches, averaging a 7.5 Sofascore rating, which could be vital in keeping Torino’s attack at bay. Venezia’s goal distribution highlights late-game threats, with 28.57% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Torino’s defensive stats are decent, conceding 40 goals this season (9th least in the league), and they’ve managed 10 clean sheets, ranking 10th. However, their attacking efficiency is middling, converting just 34.55% of big chances (14th in the league), with 36 big chances missed. Venezia, struggling offensively, might exploit Torino’s weakness in ground duels, where they rank last in the league with a 46.55% win rate. Venezia’s timing for goals late in games could test Torino, who have conceded first in 4 of their last 5 matches, potentially leaving them vulnerable if they tire.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: With referee Simone Sozza officiating, expect a match with potential disciplinary action, given his average of 4.7 yellow cards per game. Torino have accumulated 76 yellow cards this season (15th least in the league), averaging 2.17 per game, while their matches recently have seen under 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 home games. On the set-piece front, Torino average 4.09 corners per game (17th in the league), and Venezia’s away games often feature under 10.5 corners, suggesting a game with limited corner opportunities.
